Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Fun with CSF firmware (RK3588 GPU firmware) 52 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Algorithm Visualizer 160 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. Microbenchmarking Chipsets for Giggles 32 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Show HN: DuckDB community extension for prefiltered HNSW using ACORN-1 77 upvotes, 5 comments
  5. Show HN: I took back Video.js after 16 years and we rewrote it to be 88% smaller 507 upvotes, 99 comments
  6. VNDB founder Yorhel has died 110 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Show HN: Email.md – Markdown to responsive, email-safe HTML 342 upvotes, 85 comments
  8. TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression 330 upvotes, 91 comments
  9. Wine 11 rewrites how Linux runs Windows games at kernel with massive speed gains 1125 upvotes, 401 comments
  10. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 31 upvotes, 5 comments
  11. The Last Testaments of Richard II and Henry IV 49 upvotes, 11 comments
  12. Flighty Airports 420 upvotes, 150 comments
  13. My Astrophotography in the Movie Project Hail Mary 188 upvotes, 68 comments
  14. I wanted to build vertical SaaS for pest control, so I took a technician job 368 upvotes, 150 comments
  15. Local LLM App by Ente 149 upvotes, 56 comments
  16. Antimatter has been transported for the first time 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. OpenAI's latest repo has Claude as the third top contributor 50 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. Tell HN: Litellm 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 on PyPI are compromised 818 upvotes, 455 comments
  19. Meta told to pay $375M for misleading users over child safety 359 upvotes, 188 comments
  20. Apple Business 682 upvotes, 381 comments
  21. VitruvianOS – Desktop Linux Inspired by the BeOS 256 upvotes, 155 comments
  22. Goodbye to Sora 916 upvotes, 669 comments
  23. You can run a DNS server (2025) 117 upvotes, 76 comments
  24. Arm AGI CPU 379 upvotes, 282 comments
  25. Why I forked httpx 185 upvotes, 137 comments
  26. Apple Just Lost Me 264 upvotes, 226 comments
  27. Building a coding agent in Swift from scratch 19 upvotes, 9 comments
  28. Looking at Unity made me understand the point of C++ coroutines 89 upvotes, 77 comments
  29. How to Keep ICE Agents Out of Your Devices at Airports 44 upvotes, 33 comments
  30. In Edison’s Revenge, Data Centers Are Transitioning From AC to DC 211 upvotes, 254 comments
